---
title: "Research"
description: "Research projects by Bladimir Padilla on multilevel analysis, causal inference, and Chilean higher education."
---

My research involves two main areas: **tools** (i.e., quantitative methods) and **topics** (i.e., substantive areas). I enjoy studying the tools I use in research, particularly their history, characteristics, advantages, and limitations. I am passionate about making these methods accessible through practical examples, and I enjoy working on tutorials and how-to projects. As for the topics, I have been primarily involved in higher education, studying performance on standardized tests and admission to selective higher education institutions.

In this section, you will find some of the projects I am currently working on.

::: {.section-title}
Working Papers
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Exploring the Probabilities of Being Admitted to Selective Universities in Chile in 2022
**Rodríguez, C., Padilla, B., Mojoyinola, M., & Romero, D.**

This paper analyzes the admission results of Chilean students who participated in the selective higher education admission process in 2022 using a binary logistic regression model with academic and non-academic predictors. The results show that non-academic attributes were relevant predictors of the probability of admission into selective universities, especially students' family income quintile and school sector, which challenges the principle of fairness among students.

::: {.tag}
Draft available upon request
:::
:::

::: {.section-title}
Works in Progress
:::

::: {.project-card}
### On the Implications of Adjusting for Confounders, Colliders, and Mediators: A Tutorial Using ANCOVA and Inverse Probability Weighting
**Padilla, B., & Aloe, A.**

This paper presents arguments and examples regarding the precautions that researchers interested in using propensity scores and ANCOVA should consider in accordance with the presence of confounders, colliders, and mediators in the structure of their causal models.

::: {.tag}
Early Stage
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Treatment Effect Terminology in Education: A Guide for Researchers, Practitioners, and Stakeholders
**Padilla, B., & Aloe, A.**

This paper introduces the core concepts and vocabulary of treatment effect estimation in the context of educational research. Starting from the potential outcomes framework, it explains how treatment effects are formally defined, what assumptions are required to estimate them, and why establishing the identifiability of a causal effect is a necessary prior step to any analytic decision.

::: {.tag}
Early Stage
:::
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Multilevel Analysis for Intensive Longitudinal Data
**Padilla, B., & Hoffman, L.**

Book chapter dedicated to multilevel modeling approaches for intensive longitudinal data, focusing on core concepts and offering recommendations on current best practices.

::: {.tag}
Early Stage
:::
:::

::: {.section-title}
Research Dissemination
:::

::: {.project-card}
### Measuring Classroom Climate in Chilean Secondary Schools: A Proposal With Multilevel Measurement Models

Summary of a research project intended to be submitted to the Fondecyt Research Initiation Grant 2028. The project originated as my Comprehensive Exam proposal to become a PhD Candidate in Psychological and Quantitative Foundations at the University of Iowa in 2025, and was presented at a regular meeting of IESED in May 2026.

<a href="crClimateIESED.html">Read more →</a>
:::

<a href="#top" class="back-to-top">↑ Back to top</a>
